question_title: How many named storms will the 2026 Eastern Pacific hurricane season have had through July 23, 2026?
summary: The 2026 EPAC season has 4 named storms as of July 13 (Amanda, Boris, Cristina, Douglas), a hard floor. NHC's operational Tropical Weather Outlook flags two strong genesis candidates east of 140°W: Area 1 offshore SW Mexico (90% 7-day genesis → likely Elida, 5th storm) and Area 5 new low off southern Mexico (60% 7-day → potential 6th). A Monte Carlo built from these probabilities (0.88, 0.58, 0.10 for a third, plus Poisson(0.35) background genesis for late-window systems) yields P(4)=3%, P(5)=29%, P(6)=46%, P(7)=18%, P(8)=4%, P(9)=0.5% — mean 5.9, median 6, mode 6. Reaching 5 is near-certain given the 90% candidate; the 6th hinges on the 60% system.

factors.description: 4 storms already formed — hard floor; count cannot be below 4
NHC Area 1 has 90% 7-day genesis — very likely 5th storm (Elida) before July 23
NHC Area 5 has 60% 7-day genesis — decent chance of 6th storm
Below-normal ACE suggests weak/sheared systems that could stall as depressions short of TS strength
Active July basin; possible late-window third genesis

reasoning:
My forecast for the number of named storms in the 2026 Eastern Pacific hurricane season through July 23, 2026:

**Median: 6 named storms** (mode 6, mean 5.9)

**Distribution:**
- 4 storms: ~3%
- 5 storms: ~29%
- 6 storms: ~46%
- 7 storms: ~18%
- 8 storms: ~4%
- 9+ storms: <1%

**Key reasoning:** The season has a hard floor of 4 (Amanda, Boris, Cristina, Douglas). As of July 13, NHC's Tropical Weather Outlook shows two strong genesis candidates east of 140°W — Area 1 offshore SW Mexico at 90% (likely to become Elida, the 5th storm) and a new low off southern Mexico at 60% (potential 6th). Reaching 5 is near-certain; the 6th depends on the second system organizing. I applied small haircuts to the NHC genesis odds since named-storm status requires reaching tropical-storm strength (39 mph), and this season's below-normal ACE hints at weaker systems that could stall as depressions.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as the cumulative number of named storms (systems assigned a name from the Eastern Pacific 2026 name list: Amanda, Boris, Cristina, Douglas, Elida, Fausto, Genevieve, Hernan, Iselle, Julio, Karina, Lowell, Marie, Norbert, and onward) in the 2026 Eastern Pacific hurricane season (basin east of 140°W) that had been named on or before July 23, 2026 (UTC), according to the U.S. National Hurricane Center. Resolution uses the season summary table at https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/data/tcr/index.php?season=2026&basin=epac — count each listed storm whose start date is July 23, 2026 or earlier. A system counts if the NHC designated it as a named tropical (or subtropical) storm at any point on or before July 23, 2026, even if it later dissipated. As of July 10, 2026, four named storms had formed (Amanda, Boris, Cristina, Douglas), so a final count of six would resolve as "6". If the NHC summary table has not been updated to reflect a storm that formed by July 23, resolution uses the tally reported by NHC advisories or other [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
fine_print: Only storms forming in the Eastern Pacific basin east of 140°W count; Central Pacific systems (west of 140°W) are excluded. A tropical or subtropical depression that never reached named-storm status does not count. Depressions that were later upgraded and named on or before July 23, 2026 (UTC) count as named storms.

factors.description: 4 named storms already formed (Amanda, Boris, Cristina, Douglas) — hard floor of 4. Count cannot be below 4.
NHC Area 1 (offshore SW Mexico) has 90% 7-day genesis odds — very likely becomes Elida (5th) well before July 23
NHC Area 5 (new low S Mexico) has 60% 7-day genesis — decent chance of a 6th storm by July 23
Climatology: 5th storm typically forms ~Jul 22-23, 6th near end July; season running ~1 storm ahead of normal
Basin climatologically active in July; background genesis could add a 3rd new storm in late window

input.anchor: Climatological ~5-6 named storms by July 23 (EPAC normal), season running ~1 ahead
input.assessment: Current count is 4 (hard floor). Monte Carlo built from NHC operational genesis probabilities: Area1 ~0.88 (Elida/5th), Area5 ~0.58 (6th), Area4 ~0.10, plus Poisson(0.35) background. Result: P(4)=3%, P(5)=29%, P(6)=46%, P(7)=18%, P(8)=4%, P(9)=0.5%. Mean 5.9, median 6. The 90% genesis candidate makes reaching 5 near-certain (~97%); the 58% second candidate pulls the mode to 6. Distribution is right-skewed with thin upper tail.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the two flagged disturbances (Area1, Area5) both reach named-storm (TS) strength by Jul 23, and whether a third late-window system forms. Genesis odds are for tropical cyclone formation; a small fraction stall as depressions.
input.tool_audit: research() returned excellent current NHC Tropical Weather Outlook data (genesis probabilities for each area of interest) — directly usable. sandbox execute_code ran the Monte Carlo cleanly. No failures.
input.process_reflection: Well-scaffolded for this question type. The research sub-agent surfaced exactly the operational data needed (NHC genesis odds per disturbance), which is the key input. Monte Carlo from Bernoulli candidates + Poisson background was straightforward. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all five factors against the research trace; no material errors found.

GROUNDING: All factors trace to real tool results.
- Factor 1 (4-storm floor): NHC archive confirms EP01 Amanda, EP02 Boris, EP03 Cristina, EP04 Douglas. The resolution criteria itself states "four named storms had formed," so this floor is baked into the question. ✓
- Factor 2 (Area 1, 90%/7-day → Elida): NHC TWO fetch confirms Area 1 offshore SW Mexico, 48hr 60% / 7-day 90%. Logit +2.0 correctly pushes count up. ✓
- Factor 3 (Area 5, 60%/7-day): NHC TWO confirms new low offshore southern Mexico, 7-day 60%. ✓
- Factors 4-5 (climatology/background): modest weight (logit +0.3/+0.2); the climatology claim is not strongly sourced but low-impact and consistent with the "most active start since 1985" framing. Minor.

No hallucination, no double-counting, no wrong-direction factors, no contradictory assessment.

RESOLUTION ALIGNMENT: Criteria are fully provided and the agent engaged with the specific terms (named-storm status per NHC, ≤ July 23 UTC, east of 140°W excluded Central Pacific systems, count 4 as floor). The agent correctly noted the Central Pacific areas (2, 3) are near/west of 140°W and excluded them.

PRE-PUBLICATION: The 4 existing storms formed before publication, but the criteria explicitly incorporate them as the starting floor; the forecast builds forward-looking probability for storms 5+. No violation.

WINDOW NUANCE (checked closely): The resolution window is ~10 days (Jul 13→23) but the NHC genesis probabilities are 7-day. The agent applied small haircuts (0.90→0.88, 0.60→0.58) for the genesis-vs-named-strength gap, which roughly offsets the 3 extra days. This is a defensible, mildly conservative treatment — not a regime error.

PROBABILITY: My independent read matches the agent's. With Area 1 at 90% genesis over a 10-day window, P(≥5)≈95% is well-supported; the 6th storm hinges on the 60% Area-5 system, justifying mode 6. MC output (P4=3%, P5=29%, P6=46%, P7=18%; mean 5.9, median 6) maps cleanly to the tentative center 6.0, range 5.0–7.0.

SELF-EXAMINATION: The counterargument is substantive (genesis ≠ named-strength, below-normal ACE implying weak/sheared systems, possible merger of the two southern-Mexico systems) and names a concrete mind-changer (NHC downgrade of Area 1). Confidence 0.80 is calibrated to the genesis uncertainty. Tool audit's "no failures" matches ground-truth 0% error rate (18 calls, 0 errors).

WORLDVIEW: The related research entry (0c26c48c) is this session's own research and is consistent. Other tropical entries are Atlantic-basin (different question), so no cross-question contradiction. No numeric sub-forecast on the same EPAC quantity exists to cross-check.

No errors affecting the forecast direction or magnitude.
